vulnerability.reference: Clinician over-reliance on AI diagnostic outputs without sufficient rare-disease expertise to detect errors.
vulnerability.generated: Radiologists' and AI systems' inability to detect subtle synthetic anatomical implausibilities due to scarce real rare-disease reference data.

impact.reference: Delayed or incorrect treatment for patients with rare diseases, worsening health outcomes.
impact.generated: False-positive diagnoses trigger unnecessary invasive procedures and anxiety; false-negative diagnoses delay critical treatment, worsening patient outcomes.

dose_response.reference: Misrepresented rare-disease images cause false positives or negatives, which in AI-assisted screening exposes radiologists and patients to delayed or incorrect treatment.
dose_response.generated: Diffusion models generating out-of-distribution rare-disease samples, through AI-assisted screening of rare diseases, trigger radiologists' and AI systems' inability to detect subtle anatomical implausibilities from scarce reference data, leading to false-positive invasive procedures and false-negative treatment delays.
